evals.name: full-assessment-report-chained-context
evals.prompt:
Please generate a full AI Act Examination Report. I'm running this as the final step in a chained workflow — I've already run /ai-act-classifier, /ai-act-roles, and /ai-act-obligations on the same system. Here is the consolidated Assessment Context Block from those prior runs:

```
System: TalentScreenAI v1.2 (in-house CV-screening AI)
Provider: HR-Tech GmbH (DE, 45 employees, HR-tech SaaS sector)
Classification: High-risk under Annex III Nr. 4(a) (recruitment/selection)
Role: Provider AND Deployer (dual role — vendor uses internally for its own hiring too)
GPAI implications: None (built on top of OpenAI GPT-4 API as integrator, did not modify the GPAI itself; Art. 25(4) integrator info-flow rights apply)
Sector: HR-tech SaaS (own use) + financial services customers
Jurisdiction: Germany (primary), Austria, Switzerland (markets)
Org Size: Small enterprise (45 FTE)
Existing Frameworks: ISO 27001 underway, no QMS, no Art. 9 RMS
Obligations summary: Provider — Art. 9 RMS, Art. 17 QMS (Annex IV simplified per Art. 11(1) SME), Annex VI internal control, Art. 47 DoC, Art. 48 CE marking (digital), Art. 49 EU database registration, Art. 72 PMM, Art. 73 incident reporting. Deployer (own internal use) — Art. 26 cluster, Art. 4 AI literacy. Art. 50 transparency to candidates via deployer customers.
Compliance Deadline: 2 December 2027 (Art. 113(c) as amended by AI Omnibus 2026; Annex III applicability postponed from the original 2 August 2026)
Report reference: Prepared by GC of HR-Tech GmbH, 2026-05-12, internal client matter HR-25-001
```

We acknowledge the legal disclaimer. Please produce the FULL Assessment Report (not Prüfprotokoll, not Compliance Register, not Management Briefing — the full formal report). Should be suitable for legal files / regulatory inquiry. Cover: system description, classification + legal basis, role determination, full obligations matrix, gaps + risk register, recommended action plan, regulatory references. Phase 1.5 cross-checks should validate the inputs against the system description (no inconsistencies to catch — but show the check was done).
evals.expected_output: Full structured Examination Report following the skill's template. Should include all standard sections: executive summary, system description, classification (with legal basis), role determination, obligations matrix, gaps + risk register, action plan, regulatory citations. Phase 1.5 Input Validation should appear (even if it produces no flags, the cross-check itself should be documented as having run). Output should be suitable as a deliverable to legal files / regulator inquiry.

evals.name: phase15-catches-misclassification
evals.prompt:
I'd like an AI Act Examination Report for the system described below. We acknowledge the legal disclaimer.

```
System: HiringCopilot (in-house AI tool)
Description: HiringCopilot is an AI tool we use to evaluate job applicants — it reads applicant CVs, extracts skills/experience, scores candidates against our job descriptions, and produces a ranked shortlist for hiring managers. We deploy it in-house at our German GmbH (financial services, 1,200 employees).
Classification (stated): LIMITED RISK (Art. 50 transparency obligations only)
Role: Deployer (we built it ourselves but we don't sell it — we just use it internally)
Sector: Financial services
Jurisdiction: Germany
Org Size: Medium enterprise
```

Please produce the full Assessment Report. Note: I've classified this as 'limited risk' because we're not selling the system — it's just for our own internal hiring.
evals.expected_output: CRITICAL: Phase 1.5 Input Validation MUST flag the classification inconsistency. The stated 'limited risk' classification is wrong — a CV-screening AI for recruitment is high-risk under Annex III Nr. 4(a), regardless of internal use vs. sale. The skill should either (a) refuse to produce the report and ask user to re-run /ai-act-classifier, OR (b) produce the report with explicit ⚠ Classification Inconsistency flag at the top and a clear note that the report's content reflects high-risk classification (the correct one), not the user's stated limited-risk. Should also note the user's role analysis is wrong — building the system in-house makes them ALSO a provider (Art. 3(3)), not deployer-only. Should ideally also reference Art. 5(1)(a) prohibited if any manipulation is involved, but the system as described doesn't appear to trigger that.

evals.name: management-briefing-format
evals.prompt:
Quick request — instead of the full Assessment Report, please produce a Management Briefing format (the 2-page board-level decision document). Same case as the TalentScreenAI from our earlier work:

```
System: TalentScreenAI v1.2 (in-house CV-screening AI)
Classification: High-risk Annex III Nr. 4(a)
Role: Provider + Deployer
Key gaps: No QMS, no Art. 9 RMS, no Annex IV docs yet
Deadline: 2 December 2027 (Art. 113(c) Annex III applicability as postponed by AI Omnibus 2026)
Org Size: Small enterprise (45 FTE), DE/AT/CH markets
```

The audience is our supervisory board (Aufsichtsrat) — they need to approve the compliance budget and timeline. Focus on: business risk if not compliant (penalties, market access blocked, reputation), required investment, critical decision points for the board. Keep to ~2 pages of board-ready material. Acknowledge legal disclaimer.
evals.expected_output: Concise 2-page Management Briefing format. Should include: (1) executive summary / TL;DR, (2) business risk (penalties up to EUR 15M / 3% turnover under Art. 99(4); market access blocked without CE marking; reputational risk), (3) required investment (FTE allocation, external counsel, tooling, phased ~18-month programme to the 2 Dec 2027 Annex III applicability deadline per AI Omnibus 2026), (4) critical decisions the board must take (e.g., approve budget, allocate AI Officer, sign off on FRIA/DPIA, decide whether to suspend EU market access if slipping), (5) timeline gates. Should NOT be the full 10+ page Assessment Report — must respect the 'Management Briefing format' request.
